mechanism_narrative: Increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect rising labor income that supports aggregate consumption and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive changes in PAYEMS coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% anchors the risk-free rate at the zero lower bound, reducing discount rates on future earnings and forcing yield-seeking capital into equities, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and retail sales demonstrate that supply-side output is being absorbed by final demand, reducing inventory accumulation risk and lifting earnings expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint upward moves in INDPRO and RSAFS fail to predict positive 20-day returns, producing a rank correlation below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the early stage of an economic recovery, rising housing starts signal pent-up demand release and construction multiplier effects that boost cyclical earnings, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rising HOUST coincides with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of observations during post-recession recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and positive operating leverage that lifts near-term earnings surprises, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following increases in INDPRO are negative more than 50% of the time over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 6% signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income growth and triggers recession-driven earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising consumer prices and falling nonfarm payrolls reflect stagflationary pressure that compresses real incomes and profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction of rising CPIAUCSL and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 60-day returns, yielding a t-statistic below 1.5 in rolling regressions over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP confirms the persistence of aggregate demand and lowers recession probability, prompting risk-on repositioning that generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarterly releases.
